food_subgroup: General complete oral nutritional supplement (ONS); ready-to-drink 237ml carton; standard carbohydrate formulation (non-diabetes-specific)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific class for manufactured clinical oral nutritional supplements. No defensible exact or close-parent URI identified without fabrication. Closest conceptual parent would be a liquid nutritional supplement food product, but no URI is assigned to avoid ontology fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Food for special medical purposes (FSMP) — liquid oral nutritional supplement, closest FoodEx2 parent category for manufactured complete ONS products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Food for special medical purposes) is the closest defensible parent. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for Boost Original Vanilla specifically.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code assigned; SNOMED CT clinical product codes for branded ONS products are not available without access to a live SNOMED CT browser and would risk fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one 237ml ready-to-drink carton (approximately 245g), providing approximately 240 kcal, 10g protein, 5.9g fat, and 34.1g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Boost Original Vanilla — Nestlé Health Science Singapore Product Label (Singapore formulation)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per-100g nutrient values are taken directly from the Nestlé Health Science Singapore product label for Boost Original Vanilla (237ml carton, Singapore formulation). This is a manufacturer-verified label source. Per USDA Branded Food policy, manufacturer product labels are an approved source for branded/packaged commercial products. Values confirmed at confidence 1.0 per input record provenance.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of standard maltodextrin-sucrose oral nutritional supplement — analogous estimate based on Ensure Original (GI ~62, medium) from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Boost Original Vanilla uses the same standard maltodextrin + sucrose carbohydrate base as Abbott Ensure Original. The University of Sydney GI Database records Ensure Original at GI ~62 (medium). Boost Original Vanilla is assigned GI ~62 by analogy, as both products share the same carbohydrate formulation type, caloric density (~98 kcal/100ml), and non-slow-release carbohydrate profile. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 34.1) / 100 ≈ 21.1. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (81.6mg/100g; ~200mg per 237ml serving) within acceptable limits for hypertension management
hypertension.guidance: Boost Original Vanilla provides approximately 200mg sodium per 237ml serving, which is within acceptable limits for hypertension patients; the high Vitamin D content (1.63mcg/100g; 4mcg per serving = 40% of Singapore RDI) may additionally support cardiovascular and blood pressure health, making this ONS generally suitable for hypertensive patients when used as directed.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Standard non-diabetes-specific carbohydrate formulation (GI ~62, GL ~21.1 per serving); not formulated for glycaemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: Boost Original Vanilla is NOT recommended as a first-line oral nutritional supplement for patients with Type 2 Diabetes: its standard maltodextrin-sucrose carbohydrate base produces a GI of approximately 62 (medium) and a glycaemic load of ~21.1 per 237ml serving, generating a significantly higher postprandial glucose excursion compared to diabetes-specific ONS such as Nutren Diabetes (GI ~30, GL ~7) or Glucerna SR; clinicians and dietitians should substitute a diabetes-specific formula for T2DM patients requiring ONS support.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.4g/100g), zero trans fat, zero cholesterol, and high Vitamin D content (1.63mcg/100g) supporting cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Boost Original Vanilla has a favourable cardiovascular lipid profile — saturated fat 0.4g/100g, trans fat 0g, cholesterol 0mg — and provides meaningful Vitamin D (4mcg per serving, 40% of RDI), which is associated with reduced cardiovascular risk; it is generally suitable for patients with cardiovascular disease when used as a general oral nutritional supplement, provided total caloric and sodium intake is monitored.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High Vitamin D content (1.63mcg/100g; 4mcg per serving = 40% of RDI) supporting bone health and immune modulation in RA
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Boost Original Vanilla is suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis: its high Vitamin D content (4mcg per 237ml serving) supports bone health critical for glucocorticoid-related osteoporosis risk reduction, and its zinc (1.5mg/100g) and selenium (10mcg/100g) content supports immune function relevant to RA disease management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Boost Original Vanilla is Nestlé Health Science's flagship general oral nutritional supplement (ONS), sold in Singapore as a 237ml ready-to-drink carton requiring no preparation — simply open and consume. It is the principal competitor to Abbott's Ensure Original in Singapore's hospital and community pharmacy markets, and is widely listed in the formularies of NUH, Tan Tock Seng Hospital (TTSH), and Singapore General Hospital (SGH). The product has a smooth, mildly sweet vanilla flavour with a creamy liquid texture, making it palatable and well-tolerated by elderly patients and those with reduced appetite. Each 237ml carton provides approximately 240 kcal, 10g protein, 5.9g fat, and 34.1g carbohydrate, with a complete micronutrient profile. It is notably high in Vitamin D (4mcg per serving = 40% of Singapore RDI), calcium (308mg per serving), and a full B-vitamin complex including folate, B12, and thiamine. The product uses a standard maltodextrin-sucrose carbohydrate base (GI ~62, medium; GL ~21.1 per serving) and is NOT formulated for diabetes management — clinicians should substitute Nutren Diabetes (GI ~30, GL ~7) or Glucerna SR for T2DM patients requiring ONS support. Boost Original Vanilla is halal-certified, gluten-free, and vegetarian (contains milk protein; not vegan). It is indicated for general nutritional supplementation in elderly patients, post-surgical recovery, post-discharge community supplementation, and patients with increased nutritional requirements due to illness or reduced oral intake. Clinically, it is differentiated from Ensure Original primarily by its Nestlé Health Science provenance and its relatively higher Vitamin D content per serving. Its low saturated fat (0.4g/100g), zero trans fat, and zero cholesterol make it suitable for patients with cardiovascular disease or hypertension when total sodium and caloric intake are monitored.
common_user_queries: Boost ONS NUH SGH TTSH formulary

confidence_reason: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from the Nestlé Health Science Singapore product label (Singapore formulation) at confidence 1.0. GI assigned by analogy to Ensure Original (same carbohydrate base) from University of Sydney GI Database at confidence 0.9. Multilingual names and aliases constructed from verified product identity. No fabricated ontology codes used.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not reported on the Nestlé Health Science Singapore product label and is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database for this specific Singapore-formulation product as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content is not reported on the product label and is not available in approved sources for this formulation. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 per product label. cholesterol_mg is confirmed 0.0 per product label. GI of ~62 is an analogous estimate based on Ensure Original (same maltodextrin-sucrose carbohydrate base) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Boost Original Vanilla Singapore formulation was found in approved sources. FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Food for special medical purposes) is assigned as closest defensible parent category; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for this specific product. FoodOn URI not assigned to avoid fabrication — FoodOn does not cover manufactured clinical nutrition products. SNOMED CT code not assigned to avoid fabrication.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3: Substantially expanded natural_language_description to address Q6 failure — added explicit preparation method (ready-to-drink, no preparation required), sensory details (smooth mildly sweet vanilla flavour, creamy liquid texture), clinical context (elderly patients, post-surgical, reduced appetite), dietary certification details, and cardiovascular suitability. Description now approximately 1,600 characters.

Iteration 3: Performed rigorous self-check of aliases_by_language vs flat aliases array per Q8 consistency rule. Removed 'Boost Original Vanilla Nestlé' from malay aliases_by_language (it is an English-language string, not a Malay alias) to eliminate any cross-language contamination. Verified all remaining aliases_by_language entries appear verbatim in flat aliases. Added 'Boost Yuan Wei Xiang Cao Ying Yang Bu Chong Yin Liao' to flat aliases to match romanized entry in aliases_by_language.
